Enforc:etHllt 
322-6441 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-l22 
Dear Ms. Johnson: 
Thank you for your letter requesting advice concerning your 
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Political Reform Act.ll 
You are a member of the governing board of the ~eef-Sunset 
Unified School District. 
Your husband is employed as a teacher 
by the. District. 
You have asked whether, due to your husband's 
employment with the District, you are required to disqualify 
yourself from participating in the governing board's decision on 
the 1985-86 agreement between the District and the local 
teachers' association. 
I have enclosed a copy of a letter we wrote to Robert 
Calfee (our file No. A-84-227) in response to a question very 
similar to the one you have posed. 
In the Calfee letter, we 
concluded that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a 
school district governing board member from participating in 
decisions affecting the salaries of the district's teachers when 
his or her spouse is employed as a teacher in the district. 
I 
have also enclosed a copy of our letter to Margaret O'Donnell 
(our file No. A-84-044) which confirms our advice to Dr. Calfee. 
Our advice is confined to the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act and does not cover provisions of the Education Code 
or other laws which may affect your ability to participate in 
11 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 


} 
! 
Joan Johnson 
June 17, 1985 
Page 2 
the governing board's decisions. 
You may wish to consult with 
the County Counselor the school district's attorney for advice 
as to any other applicable laws. 
If you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours,

I am an appointed member of the Reef-Sunset Unified 
School District Governing Board. 
My husband is employed 
by the district as a teacher. 
Before my tem expires, the board will be voting on 
the 1985-86 agreement between the district and the local 
teachers' association (teachers' contract), which, among 
other things, affects my spouse's salary. 
What guidelines should I follow in this situation? 
Is it proper for me to abstain from voting? I would 
appreciate knowing the position of the Commission in 
this regard.

sections.question: I am an appointed member of the Reef-Sunset Unified School District Governing Board. My husband is employed by the district as a teacher. Before my term expires, the board will be voting on the 1985-86 agreement between the district and the local teachers' association (teachers' contract), which, among other things, affects my spouse's salary. What guidelines should I follow in this situation? Is it proper for me to abstain from voting? I would appreciate knowing the position of the Commission in this regard.
sections.conclusion: In the Calfee letter, we concluded that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a school district governing board member from participating in decisions affecting the salaries of the district's teachers when his or her spouse is employed as a teacher in the district.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a school district governing board member must disqualify herself from voting on a teachers' contract negotiation when her spouse is employed as a teacher in the district.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit participation in decisions affecting teacher salaries when the board member's spouse is employed as a teacher in the district. However, other state laws may impose additional restrictions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (May 19, 1985) and FPPC response (June 17, 1985). Response relies on prior advice letters (A-84-227 and A-84-044) rather than providing independent analysis. FPPC explicitly limits advice to Political Reform Act provisions only.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I am an appointed member of the Reef-Sunset Unified School District Governing Board. My husband is employed by the district as a teacher. Before my term expires, the board will be voting on the 1985-86 agreement between the district and the local teachers' association (teachers' contract), which, among other things, affects my spouse's salary. What guidelines should I follow in this situation? Is it proper for me to abstain from voting? I would appreciate knowing the position of the Commission in this regard.

CONCLUSION: In the Calfee letter, we concluded that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit a school district governing board member from participating in decisions affecting the salaries of the district's teachers when his or her spouse is employed as a teacher in the district.

Before my tem expires, the board will be voting on the 1985-86 agreement between the district and the local teachers' association (teachers' contract), which, among other things, affects my spouse's salary. embedding.summary: FPPC advises that the Political Reform Act does not require disqualification from voting on a teachers' contract when the board member's spouse is a district teacher, referencing prior advice letters with similar conclusions. The respondent is advised to consult other counsel regarding Education Code provisions.
